verses.1: God of my praise, don’t remain silent,
verses.2: for they have opened the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of deceit against me.
verses.3: They have also surrounded me with words of hatred,
verses.4: In return for my love, they are my adversaries;
verses.5: They have rewarded me evil for good,
verses.6: Set a wicked man over him.
verses.7: When he is judged, let him come out guilty.
verses.10: Let his children be wandering beggars.
verses.11: Let the creditor seize all that he has.
verses.12: Let there be no one to extend kindness to him,
verses.13: Let his posterity be cut off.
verses.14: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered by the LORD.
verses.15: Let them be before the LORD continually,
verses.16: because he didn’t remember to show kindness,
verses.17: Yes, he loved cursing, and it came to him.
verses.18: He clothed himself also with cursing as with his garment.
verses.19: Let it be to him as the clothing with which he covers himself,
verses.20: This is the reward of my adversaries from the LORD,
verses.21: But deal with me, GOD the Lord, for your name’s sake,
verses.22: for I am poor and needy.
verses.23: I fade away like an evening shadow.
verses.24: My knees are weak through fasting.
verses.25: I have also become a reproach to them.
verses.27: that they may know that this is your hand;
verses.28: They may curse, but you bless.
verses.29: Let my adversaries be clothed with dishonor.
verses.30: I will give great thanks to the LORD with my mouth.
verses.31: For he will stand at the right hand of the needy,
